expectations.text: Adds kelet to pyproject.toml or requirements
expectations.evidence: kelet>=1.3.0 was already present in pyproject.toml before the integration; the skill did not add it and the diff contains no change to pyproject.toml.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() is called at startup (not per-request)
expectations.evidence: app/main.py diff adds kelet.configure() at module level before the lifespan/app definition, exactly once at startup.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps the agent call with the existing Redis session_id
expectations.evidence: chat.py diff shows async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id) wrapping chat_agent.iter(), using the Redis-stored UUID from cache/__init__.py.

expectations.text: Does not add VoteFeedback or React (app has no React frontend)
expectations.evidence: Transcript explicitly rules out VoteFeedback and React; the diff has no React or feedback-ui changes at all.

expectations.text: KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT written to .env (not hardcoded in source)
expectations.evidence: .env.example diff adds KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT as env var placeholders with instructions; neither value is hardcoded in source files.

expectations.text: AskUserQuestion called at most 3 times total across the whole integration
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows exactly 2 checkpoints (mapping confirmation and plan+inputs), both within the 3-question budget.

expectations.text: Integration is lightweight — does not add unnecessary signals or complexity
expectations.evidence: Only configure() and agentic_session() added; no coded signals, no new UI, no extra dependencies — transcript explicitly justifies omitting signals as correct for lightweight mode.

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator deeplink is generated via Bash execution (not shown as copy-paste code block)
expectations.evidence: Transcript section 'Deeplink generated (Bash execution)' presents the fully-resolved console URL with base64url payload, not generation code or a shell snippet to copy-paste.

expectations.text: Surfaces the issue that phone number outlives sessions and cannot be used as session_id
expectations.evidence: Analysis Pass section explicitly invokes the SKILL.md rule ('User identity ≠ session ID') and Checkpoint 1 directly flags to the developer that phone should be user identity, not session_id.

expectations.text: Proposes generating a UUID per conversation (not using phone as session_id)
expectations.evidence: Transcript confirms the code already generates uuid.uuid4() per conversation and the skill endorses this pattern as correct, explicitly stating phone must NOT be session_id.

expectations.text: Passes stable identifier (phone) as user_id= in agentic_session()
expectations.evidence: The diff shows agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id) with no user_id= argument; the transcript only mentions user_id=phone_number as a future optional enhancement and does not add it to the code.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() is still added at startup
expectations.evidence: app/main.py diff adds kelet.configure() at module level before the lifespan definition.

expectations.text: Correctly identifies missing agentic_session() as the cause of unlinked traces
expectations.evidence: Transcript explicitly states 'agentic_session() is absent' as Finding 2 and explains that without it each LLM call produces an unlinked trace — exactly the reported symptom.

expectations.text: Adds agentic_session() wrapping the agent call
expectations.evidence: diff adds 'async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id):' wrapping the entire _run_agent_stream body including the [DONE] sentinel in src/routers/chat.py.

expectations.text: Does not re-ask for API keys or project name already configured. Fixing a broken/wrong method is acceptable and does NOT count as redundant.
expectations.evidence: Transcript explicitly notes .env already has the correct keys and settings model already has the fields; no prompts for new keys or project name are issued. Adding kelet.configure() is a fix, not a re-ask.

expectations.text: Includes verification step: trigger LLM call and check console for sessions
expectations.evidence: Phase V checklist includes an unchecked smoke test item: 'trigger POST /chat → open Kelet console → verify session appears (allow a few minutes)'.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() added at server startup
expectations.evidence: diff adds 'kelet.configure()' at module level in app/main.py (after imports, before lifespan), which runs once at startup.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps agent call with session_id
expectations.evidence: diff wraps 'chat_agent.iter()' with 'async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id):' in _run_agent_stream in src/routers/chat.py.

expectations.text: KeletProvider added at React root with publishable key
expectations.evidence: frontend/src/main.tsx adds KeletProvider with apiKey={import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY} and project={import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PROJECT} wrapping the App.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback component added next to AI message output (not a floating widget or separate page)
expectations.evidence: VoteFeedback.Root/UpvoteButton/DownvoteButton/Popover/Textarea/SubmitButton added inside AssistantMessage component in App.tsx, rendered inline with the assistant message content.

expectations.text: Session ID from X-Session-ID response header is captured in React state and passed to VoteFeedback sessionId prop
expectations.evidence: App.tsx reads 'res.headers.get("X-Session-ID")' into sessionId state, and AssistantMessage receives sessionId as a prop which is passed to VoteFeedback.Root session_id={sessionId}.

expectations.text: Both KELET_API_KEY and VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY are present in .env at end of integration. Neither hardcoded in source.
expectations.evidence: KELET_API_KEY is added to .env.example (backend); VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY is added to frontend/.env. Both use placeholder values, never hardcoded in source files.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback is placed and configured to look natural in the chat UI — not a jarring out-of-place widget.
expectations.evidence: frontend_changes.md confirms VoteFeedback uses existing styles.iconBtn CSS class and the app's dark palette (#1e2130, #2d3748, #e2e8f0) matching App.module.css; no new CSS classes added.

expectations.text: Skill identifies at least one additional implicit frontend signal beyond VoteFeedback — e.g. useKeletSignal for copy-to-clipboard, message retry, or session abandon.
expectations.evidence: Signal analysis proposes and implements a copy-to-clipboard button with useKeletSignal (trigger_name: 'user-copy', kind: 'EVENT', source: 'HUMAN') on each assistant message.

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator deeplink generated via Bash (not shown as code block)
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows the deeplink URL presented inline as a clickable URL (not in a code block), with the note '(executed via Bash, not shown as code block)'.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() added at server startup
expectations.evidence: changes.diff NOTE section confirms kelet.configure() was already present in app/main.py on this branch — no backend changes were needed. The string 'kelet.configure(' appears in the diff file confirming its presence.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps agent call with session_id
expectations.evidence: changes.diff NOTE section confirms kelet.agentic_session() was already in place in src/routers/chat.py wrapping the agent stream. Transcript Phase 0a analysis confirms 'kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id) wrapping chat_agent.iter() — complete: session context spans the entire streaming generator'.

expectations.text: KeletProvider added at React root with publishable key
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows KeletProvider imported and added in frontend/src/main.tsx with apiKey={import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY} and project={import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PROJECT}, wrapping App inside React.StrictMode.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback component added next to AI message output (not a floating widget or separate page)
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows VoteFeedback.Root with UpvoteButton (asChild), DownvoteButton (asChild), Popover, Textarea, and SubmitButton added inside AssistantMessage component in frontend/src/App.tsx — inline next to each assistant message, not a floating widget.

expectations.text: Session ID from X-Session-ID header passed to VoteFeedback session_id prop
expectations.evidence: frontend_changes.md documents the full end-to-end flow: server returns X-Session-ID header, React captures it via res.headers.get('X-Session-ID') and stores in sessionId state, then VoteFeedback.Root receives session_id={sessionId}. changes.diff line 74 shows 'const sid = res.headers.get(X-Session-ID); if (sid) setSessionId(sid)' and line 18 shows 'VoteFeedback.Root session_id={sessionId}'.

expectations.text: Both KELET_API_KEY and VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY present in .env (pre-existing or added)?
expectations.evidence: changes.diff NOTE confirms '.env already contained KELET_API_KEY, KELET_PROJECT, VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, and VITE_KELET_PROJECT'. frontend_changes.md line 84 confirms 'KELET_API_KEY + KELET_PROJECT + VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY + VITE_KELET_PROJECT already set'. Assertion allows pre-existing keys — both are present.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback styled to match dark theme using existing CSS classes rather than arbitrary inline styles or emoji
expectations.evidence: Vote buttons use asChild pattern with className={styles.iconBtn} (existing CSS module class), SubmitButton uses className={styles.sendBtn} (existing class matching the main Send button). Popover uses inline styles that precisely match the app's dark-theme palette (#1e2130 background, #2d3748 border — same values defined in App.module.css). No emoji. The skill explicitly noted 'No emoji, no arbitrary inline styles' for the button layer, and the popover inline styles are theme-consistent, not arbitrary.

expectations.text: Does transcript/frontend_changes.md propose useFeedbackState or useKeletSignal for at least one interaction beyond VoteFeedback?
expectations.evidence: Transcript signal analysis section explicitly states 'No copy button, no retry, no edit-in-place, no session reset button' and concludes 'VoteFeedback is the only viable coded signal'. No useFeedbackState or useKeletSignal was proposed for any additional interaction. The skill stopped at VoteFeedback without surfacing additional signal opportunities.

expectations.text: deeplink.txt contains a real https://console.kelet.ai URL?
expectations.evidence: deeplink.txt contains 'https://console.kelet.ai/docs-ai-assistant/synthetics/setup?deeplink=eyJ1c2VfY2FzZSI6...' — a real console.kelet.ai URL with base64url-encoded payload for Task Completion and Sentiment Analysis evaluators. Transcript shows deeplink was generated via Bash execution (not a code block).

expectations.text: Does not re-add configure() or agentic_session() — skips already-done work
expectations.evidence: Transcript opens with 'What Was Already Done' listing configure() and agentic_session() as correct; diff makes no changes to app/main.py or src/routers/chat.py.

expectations.text: Proposes at least one additional coded signal beyond what's already in place
expectations.evidence: Two new coded signals are proposed and implemented: user-copy (copy-to-clipboard via useKeletSignal) and user-abandon (new chat button with score 0.0), both added to App.tsx.

expectations.text: Proposes more synthetic evaluators than the default Task Completion alone
expectations.evidence: Synthetic evaluator table proposes four evaluators: Task Completion, Conversation Completeness, Knowledge Retention, and Sentiment Analysis, with a deeplink to activate all four.

expectations.text: Does not propose VoteFeedback or React since app has no React frontend
expectations.evidence: The app already has React and VoteFeedback from a prior integration; the skill does not propose adding either from scratch — it extends the existing React frontend without re-proposing VoteFeedback.

expectations.text: Explains what each new signal or evaluator catches — not just lists them
expectations.evidence: Each signal has an explicit 'Why' section (copy = 'user found the answer useful enough to act on', abandon = 'strongest implicit abandonment signal'), and each synthetic evaluator has a Rationale column explaining what failure mode it detects.
